question: [File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getMessageByConsensusTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted consensusTimestamp path parameter that matches a transaction with result=22 and type=27 to retrieve the full topic message payload of any topic without any topic-level access control, thereby gossiping the content of that transaction to unauthorized parties?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getMessageByConsensusTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all valid consensusTimestamp values by iterating nanosecond-precision timestamps in the path parameter, causing the mirror node to confirm or deny the existence of every submitted transaction and leaking the full message body of matched HCS transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validateConsensusTimestampParam()] Can an unprivileged external user bypass timestamp validation in validateConsensusTimestampParam() by supplying a timestamp string that satisfies the regex /^\\d{1,10}$/ or /^\\d{1,10}\\.\\d{1,9}$/ but resolves to a timestamp in the far future or far past, causing the DB query to silently return no rows and leaking timing information about when transactions were submitted?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getMessageByConsensusTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hardcoded filter `result = 22 and type = 27` in the transaction lookup query to confirm whether a specific nanosecond timestamp corresponds to a successful CONSENSUSSUBMITMESSAGE transaction, effectively gossiping the existence of that transaction without retrieving the message body?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getMessageByTopicAndSequenceRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user retrieve any topic message by supplying a valid topicId and sequenceNumber in the path, with no authentication or authorization check, thereby gossiping the full message content of any HCS transaction submitted to that topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validateGetSequenceMessageParams()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sequenceNumber of '1' through '9223372036854775807' (the full positive long range) to enumerate every message ever submitted to a topic, since validateGetSequenceMessageParams() only checks isPositiveLong() with no upper bound restriction, enabling complete gossip of all topic transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getMessageByTopicAndSequenceRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of topic existence validation in getMessageByTopicAndSequenceRequest() to probe arbitrary topicId values and determine which topic IDs have messages, leaking the set of active HCS topics and their transaction counts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getTopicMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user call getTopicMessages() for any topicId without any access control check, retrieving all messages (transactions) ever submitted to that topic and gossiping the full transaction history to unauthorized parties?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getTopicMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user use the pagination link (links.next) returned by getTopicMessages() to iteratively retrieve all topic messages beyond the default limit, effectively bypassing the defaultLimit restriction and gossiping the complete set of transactions for a topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply both sequencenumber and timestamp filters simultaneously to extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest() to narrow down and pinpoint specific transactions, enabling targeted gossip of individual transactions without knowing their exact consensus timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a limit parameter equal to the maximum allowed value to force getTopicMessages() to return the largest possible batch of topic messages in a single response, maximizing the volume of transaction data gossiped per request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply order=desc combined with a large limit to extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest() to retrieve the most recent transactions first, enabling real-time gossip of the latest HCS transactions submitted to any topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getRangeFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply conflicting sequencenumber filters (e.g., sequencenumber=gte:1&sequencenumber=lte:9223372036854775806) to getRangeFromFilters() to produce a maximum-width range, causing getTopicMessageTimestampRanges() to scan the entire topic_message_lookup table and return all timestamp ranges, enabling bulk gossip of all transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getRangeFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the ne operator rejection path in getRangeFromFilters() (which throws InvalidArgumentError for ne operator) to fingerprint the server's filter processing logic and infer which filter combinations are valid for crafting gossip-enabling queries?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getTopicMessageTimestampRanges()] Can an unprivileged external user supply sequencenumber filters whose computed range size (upper - lower + 1n) exceeds the limit, forcing getTopicMessageTimestampRanges() to execute the expensive CTE query path with actual_range expansion, causing a denial-of-service that prevents legitimate nodes from gossiping transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getTopicMessageTimestampRanges()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sequencenumber range where upper - lower + 1n equals exactly the limit value to force the simple query path in getTopicMessageTimestampRanges(), bypassing the CTE expansion logic and retrieving timestamp ranges for the maximum number of transactions in a single lookup?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getTopicMessageTimestampRanges()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a very large limit value combined with a wide sequencenumber range to cause the rangeExpression in getTopicMessageTimestampRanges() to expand the actual_range by up to `limit` rows, causing the second query to scan a disproportionately large portion of topic_message_lookup and return timestamp ranges covering many more transactions than intended?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getTopicMessageTimestampRanges()] Can an unprivileged external user supply order=asc with a large limit to trigger the int8range expansion `upper(range) + least($4, 9223372036854775807::bigint - upper(range))` in getTopicMessageTimestampRanges(), causing the timestamp range to expand far beyond the actual matching messages and returning timestamp ranges that cover unrelated transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getTopicMessageTimestampRanges()] Can an unprivileged external user supply order=desc with a large limit to trigger the `greatest(lower(range) - ($4 - 1), lower($2::int8range))` expansion in getTopicMessageTimestampRanges(), causing the lower bound of the actual_range to expand backward and include timestamp ranges for transactions outside the requested sequence number window?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getTopicMessageTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user call getMessageByTopicAndSequenceRequest() with topicMessageLookup enabled to trigger getTopicMessageTimestampRange(), which queries topic_message_lookup with `sequence_number_range @> $2::bigint`, and use the NotFoundError response to confirm whether a specific sequence number exists in the lookup table, enabling transaction existence gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getTopicMessageTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getTopicMessageTimestampRange() returns rows[0].timestamp_range without checking if multiple rows exist, and if the lookup table has overlapping ranges for the same sequence number, cause the function to silently return only the first range, potentially missing transactions and creating inconsistent gossip prevention?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getMessage() function's strict `rows.length !== 1` check to perform a timing-based oracle attack: if the query returns 0 rows a NotFoundError is thrown quickly, but if it returns 1 row the TopicMessageViewModel construction takes longer, leaking whether a specific transaction exists?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the zeroRandomPageCostQueryHint (`set local random_page_cost = 0`) path in getTopicMessages() by supplying a limit != 1, causing PostgreSQL to prefer the (topic_id, consensus_timestamp) index over the primary key, and exploit this index preference to perform faster bulk scans of topic messages for gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getTopicMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user supply limit=1 to getTopicMessages() to suppress the zeroRandomPageCostQueryHint, forcing PostgreSQL to use the primary key index instead of the topic index, and use the resulting slower response time as a side-channel to infer the density of transactions in a topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple sequencenumber filters (e.g., sequencenumber=gte:1&sequencenumber=lte:100&sequencenumber=gte:50) to extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest() where each filter is independently appended to conditions[], causing the SQL WHERE clause to contain redundant conditions that the DB optimizer may handle differently, potentially leaking query plan information?
